passes.notes: Read copied rule-core-exact-damage-projection.mbt.qnt, spell-damage-projection-core.qnt, attack-damage-composition.qnt, damage-component-adjustments.qnt, rule-core-component-route.qnt, and the local SRD Playing-the-Game Damage Rolls, Critical Hits, Saving Throws and Damage, Half Damage, Damage Types, and Hit Points passages.

Runtime vocabulary uses Damage Type, Critical Hit, Attack Roll, Saving Throw, and Hit Points from UBIQUITOUS_LANGUAGE.md. Component owner names preserve spell-procedure profile ownership and Hit Point damage ownership.

Component owner route facts are generated by exact_damage_component_route, localizing the two-owner route sequence. Damage projection math and Hit Point application are separate helpers matching copied rule-core ownership.
No durable state was added. Witness facts are projected at the component adapter boundary, and Hit Point application reuses the existing generic HP reducer surface.

Production code dispatches on typed damage facts, Attack Roll facts, Saving Throw policy, and component owner. It does not branch on selected spell id, name, slug, source heading, page ref, or catalog identity.
Focused tests compare computed replay against literal witness facts for all four component branches and assert both copied component owners are present.

Second pass after focused replay, full cargo test, clippy, and evidence validation found no reasonable additional findings. Selected attack-spell and level-one damage selected drivers remain explicitly unaccepted by this component-first lane.
